finding: runner-sync build timing currently appears as terminal output, ad hoc operator measurement, or git-tracked receipts, not as a first-class gitignored telemetry stream for trend analysis.
expectedBehavior: Every runner-sync build should emit structured timing telemetry with cacheHitSkip, incrementalBuild, fullRebuild, phaseTimingsMs, output file count, input/output digest, sealed source SHA, steward work id, and exit status under .atm/runtime/telemetry or another gitignored log surface. Git history should keep only compact decision digests, close summaries, or deliberately selected baselines; repeated raw build telemetry must not be committed by default.
evidenceOrRepro: During ATM-GOV-0187 closeout, npm run build produced a seed metric: totalElapsed=42004ms from .atm/history/evidence/ATM-GOV-0187.runner-sync-receipt.json, mode=cache-miss full, sealedSourceSha=313a8151abb4b09fe1fc55df53f8973f3899c7c6, stewardWorkId=runner-sync-19213019. The receipt also demonstrated why raw per-build telemetry should not accumulate in Git history.
followUp: Fold this into ATM-GOV-0194 and/or a dedicated runner telemetry task so future task cards can consume cumulative build-speed data from gitignored runtime telemetry before deciding whether to optimize, defer, or rewrite scope.
